---
title: "Jacob Johnson"
description-meta: "Learn some more about me"
image: "files/images/JacobJohnson.JPG"
page-navigation: false
toc: false
sidebar: false
page-layout: full

about:
  id: hero-heading
  template: trestles
  image-width: 70%
---

:::{#hero-heading}
# About Me

I'm a second year PhD student at the [University of Wisconsin–Madison](https://stat.wisc.edu/) working with [Dr. Matthias Katzfuß](https://sites.google.com/view/katzfuss/home). I am also a year-round statistics intern at [Sandia National Laboratories](https://www.sandia.gov/) working with [Dr. Katherine Goode](https://goodekat.github.io/).  
My research is focused on finding new methods in Spatial Statistics, Gaussian Processes, and Uncertainty Quantification, specifically with applications in climate and environmental science.  
Before starting my PhD, I did my undergrad at [Brigham Young University](https://statistics.byu.edu/) where I did research with [Dr. Matthew Heaton](https://mheaton.byu.edu/).

I'm originally from Lindon UT, and my hobbies include watching college sports, camping, hiking, and playing with my son. 

:::
